user.title: Swelligence
user.description: Choose the sports you care about and your default forecast provider. You can add favourite spots afterwards from the integration's options.

data.use_llm: Use an AI Task agent for semantic verdicts

rider.title: Rider profile & quiver
rider.description: Your weight and the kit you own drive kite/wing size recommendations and whether a spot is actually rideable for you. Enter sizes in m² separated by commas (e.g. 7, 9, 12). Leave weight blank to disable personalisation.

spot_prefs.title: Tune a spot
spot_prefs.description: Pick the spot whose per-sport preferences you want to adjust.

spot_prefs_sport.title: Choose a sport
spot_prefs_sport.description: Which sport at {spot} do you want to tune?

spot_prefs_edit.title: {sport} preferences
spot_prefs_edit.description: Tune {sport} at {spot}. Wind directions are the wind's compass origin you prefer — for surf, set the offshore direction(s) for this beach. Leave a field blank to keep the default.

data.swell_dirs: Swell window — directions this spot picks up

add_spot.title: Add a spot
add_spot.description: Type a place name to search for its coordinates, or leave it blank and enter coordinates directly (pre-filled from your Home Assistant location). The provider locks onto the nearest model grid automatically.

add_spot_pick.title: Choose a location
add_spot_pick.description: Several places matched your search for {name}. Pick the right one.

edit_spot.title: Edit a spot
edit_spot.description: Pick the spot whose sports or water type you want to change.

edit_spot_fields.title: Edit {spot}
edit_spot_fields.description: Add or remove the sports tracked at {spot}, adjust its water type, set its tide dependence, and route its data sources. Removing a sport also clears any per-sport tuning you saved for it here. Leave a source on '(use global)' to follow the integration-wide setting.

providers.title: Forecast provider API keys
providers.description: Enter API keys for the keyed providers you want to use. Leave a field blank to keep a provider disabled. Open-Meteo needs no key and always works. Tick 'Free tier' to auto-throttle polling so a free plan's daily request limit isn't exceeded.

data.marine_prefer: Prefer overlay marine over the base (else gap-fill only)
data.marine_ensemble: Cross-provider confidence (compare both marine sources; uses extra requests)
data.marine_blend: Blend the two marine sources to a consensus (needs cross-provider confidence)

data.use_llm: Use an AI Task agent for semantic verdicts

error.duplicate_spot: A spot with that name already exists.
error.geocode_no_results: No matching place found. Try a different name or enter coordinates directly.

abort.no_spots: Add a spot first before tuning preferences.
abort.no_keyed_providers: No keyed providers are available to configure.

options.mid: Best on the run (mid / max flow)
